flag_detail: Signed a one-year, $7 million deal with Tampa Bay after 2025, so this looks more like a bridge spot than a long runway.
Struggled after the 2025 trade to the Mets, hitting .182 over 42 games before reaching free agency.
Dislocated his left shoulder on an April 2025 catch and was ruled out for at least 12 weeks.
Seattle gave him a two-year extension with a 2027 club option, so he is not just a one-month placeholder.
Landed on the IL with a Grade 1 oblique strain in April 2025, with a minimum six-week absence expected.
A 2026 review flagged negative Outs Above Average in center, which could matter if Seattle needs more OF flexibility.
Finished 2025 with a 77 wRC+ and a 34.4% strikeout rate, making the bat a real question even with strong defense.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in 2024 and opened 2025 on the 60-day IL after re-signing with the Cubs.
Signed a four-year, $240 million Dodgers deal with an opt-out after two years, making him a premium MLB-level blocker.
Minnesota used him as a primary DH in 2023 to preserve his body, then moved him back to center in 2024.
